mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in new home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing housing demand cycle, which transmits to broader corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in sales and starts fails to predict negative returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 during expansion phases or produces negative returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 65% of months following inflation readings above the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive returns in more than 50% of occurrences or yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters following positive GDP growth readings, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 45% during contraction periods or shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample validation.
